programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: Most people think of deepfakes as manipulated video clips of world leaders, designed to create confusion, but the most pressing threat of deepfakes is not politics, but porn. Research shows around 96 per cent of deepfake videos are pornographic, with almost 100 per cent of them involving non-consenting women. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick Stein travels to St Ives to meet one of Britain's little-known artists, Alfred Wallis. In the shadow of Bodmin Moor Rick visits a very rare collection of medieval stained-glass windows in the church of St Neot. It is here he ticks off an item on his bucket list by taking part in a rather unusual celebration called Oak Apple Day, marking the occasion when Charles II became king.

programme.desc: Rick meets `Springwatch' presenter and biologist Gillian Burke, who takes Rick on a walk through a rare habitat called an Atlantic temperate rainforest on the banks of the Helford River. programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch A Criminal
programme.desc: A nationwide investigation begins when Dorset Police uncover an international gang who are posing as couriers to scam vulnerable pensioners out of thousands of pounds.

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch A Criminal

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers are treated to flamboyant recipes that would be fit for a king. They learn the secret behind a perfect baked Alaska and conjure up their own prized honey glazed ham. The lads are also shown a lesson or two in etiquette.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall

It is here he ticks off an item on his bucket list by taking part in a rather unusual celebration called Oak Apple Day, marking the occasion when Charles II became king.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa
programme.desc: As the series reaches it's crescendo, one of the final three celebrities will not only win the show but they will be crowned an I'm A Celebrity… legend.

programme.title: ITV News London

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Presenting the latest products live on television that can be ordered directly. The product range extends from vacuum cleaners and bath towels to sports equipment.

programme.title: The Murder Line

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Jason Atherton's Dubai Dishes
programme.desc: Jason's debut in Dubai is all things spice as he explores a traditional souk in the heart of the old town - Jason is also joined by Dubai chef Rahul Rana, who makes a chocolate that is filled with spiced beetroot curry.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James's travels around France take him to Charente, where he starts his journey in the heart of the Cognac region and pays a visit to the de La Rochefoucauld family.

programme.title: Jurassic Park - The Lost World
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: Raymond Blanc's Royal Kitchen Gardens
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc journeys to the northernly tip of Scotland and the majestic Castle of Mey that was once home to Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James drops into one of the best food and drink markets in California before cooking with some delicious local produce at a stunning vineyard. James then gets an exclusive look at the brand-new kitchen at one of the world's best restaurants, `The French Laundry', and tours their stunning kitchen garden with chef and owner Thomas Keller.

programme.title: ITV News

programme.title: Live: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents live coverage of racing from Sandown, Leicester and Haydock. Today's feature meeting of the day is at Sandown where the 2025/2026 Jumps season concludes with some top-class action including the Grade 1 Celebration Chase. Plus, there's flat racing from Leicester and Haydock. Analysis from Sir AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reporting from Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles.

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Look back at some of the most dramatic end games in Tipping History, including agonising trades and jackpot counters teetering on the edge.

programme.desc: Because they are a little short of money, and considering money notes are only little papers with drawings on them, Petit and his friends decide to create their own money and buy anything they want.

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach
programme.desc: Brush decides to start a ladies club and invites all the ladies of the forest for brunch. When Fox and Tusk try to join in, dressed as ladies, Brush soon discovers their true identity and kicks them out. Offended, Fox and Tusk decide to start a club.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras

programme.title: Fàsach na h-Eòrpa
programme.desc: For 9,000 years, the European wild sheep could not be driven from its original territory in Cyprus and Sardinia. However, Poachers almost wiped it out, but the wild ancestor of today's domestic sheep, the mouflon, is a survival artist.

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Were the ancient Scottish tribes too much for the Roman Empire? Or was Scotland simply not worth conquering? Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter looks back on three centuries of contact and conflict with the Roman invaders. Features the first Tay Bridge, the first depiction of tartan, and forgotten Roman camps that once held thirty-five thousand men.

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: Research is revealing that sharks can be sociable and intelligent, but these breakthroughs come just as many sharks face extinction. Science may now be the only way to save them.

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: One man transfixed television viewers during snooker's golden age - his name was Alex `Hurricane' Higgins. This poignant documentary charts the life of snooker's first genius, from his early days growing up in Belfast to his climb to the top of the sport as two-time world champion. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Dennis Taylor, Steve Davis, and members of the Higgins family.

programme.title: Tennis: ATP & WTA Miami Open 2025
programme.desc: Chasing a record seventh men's Miami crown, Novak Djokovic - now the oldest ATP Masters 1000 finalist - takes on Jakub Mensik, aiming to win his first tour-level trophy.

programme.title: Tennis: ATP & WTA Cincinnati Open 2025
programme.desc: Six-time Grand Slam champion Iga Swiatek faces Jasmine Paolini for the Cincinnati title. In what will be Swiatek's 13th WTA 1000 final, Paolini is competing in just her third.
